source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure Essential Contacts is Configured for Organization

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure Essential Contacts is Configured for Organization
input_row.description: It is recommended that Essential Contacts is configured to designate email addresses for Google Cloud services to notify of important technical or security information.
input_row.rationale: Many Google Cloud services, such as Cloud Billing, send out notifications to share important information with Google Cloud users. By default, these notifications are sent to members with certain Identity and Access Management (IAM) roles. With Essential Contacts, you can customize who receives notifications by providing your own list of contacts. Impact: There is no charge for Essential Contacts except for the 'Technical Incidents' category that is only available to premium support customers.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Essential Contacts by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/essential-contacts 2. Make sure the organization appears in the resource selector at the top of the page. The resource selector tells you what project, folder, or organization you are currently managing contacts for. 3. Ensure that appropriate email addresses are configured for each of the following notification categories: • Legal • Security • Suspension • Technical Alternatively, appropriate email addresses can be configured for the All notification category to receive all possible important notifications. From Google Cloud CLI 1. To list all configured organization Essential Contacts run a command: gcloud essential-contacts list --organization=<ORGANIZATION_ID> 2. Ensure at least one appropriate email address is configured for each of the following notification categories: • LEGAL • SECURITY • SUSPENSION • TECHNICAL Alternatively, appropriate email addresses can be configured for the ALL notification category to receive all possible important notifications.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Essential Contacts by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/essential-contacts 2. Make sure the organization appears in the resource selector at the top of the page. The resource selector tells you what project, folder, or organization you are currently managing contacts for. 3. Click +Add contact 4. In the Email and Confirm Email fields, enter the email address of the contact. 5. From the Notification categories drop-down menu, select the notification categories that you want the contact to receive communications for. 6. Click Save From Google Cloud CLI 1. To add an organization Essential Contacts run a command: gcloud essential-contacts create --email="<EMAIL>" \ --notification-categories="<NOTIFICATION_CATEGORIES>" \ --organization=<ORGANIZATION_ID> Default Value: By default, there are no Essential Contacts configured. In the absence of an Essential Contact, the following IAM roles are used to identify users to notify for the following categories: • Legal : roles/billing.admin • Security : roles/resourcemanager.organizationAdmin • Suspension : roles/owner • Technical : roles/owner • Technical Incidents : roles/owner References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/managing-notification-contacts
input_row.impact: There is no charge for Essential Contacts except for the 'Technical Incidents' category that is only available to premium support customers.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- The audit field provides a clear command to query the essential contacts using the Google Cloud CLI, indicating that this control can be verified programmatically.
- The control involves querying a property (essential contacts) of the GCP organization through available GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud), which are part of the standard services and configurations offered by GCP.
- The Google Cloud CLI is specifically mentioned in the audit instructions, providing a direct command (`gcloud essential-contacts list --organization=<ORGANIZATION_ID>`) to verify if the Essential Contacts are configured.
- The task is related to a resource management aspect of GCP, making it suitable for automated verification.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_organization_essential_contacts_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) with the CLI command `gcloud essential-contacts list --organization=<ORGANIZATION_ID>` to obtain the list of essential contacts configured for a specific organization.
- Enumerate the Essential Contacts configuration for the specified organization.
- Validate that at least one email address exists for each of the required notification categories: LEGAL, SECURITY, SUSPENSION, and TECHNICAL. Alternatively, ensure there is a contact for the ALL category, which covers all possible notifications.
- Configuration properties/settings to validate include the presence of emails appropriately assigned to the notification categories.
- Pass/Fail Criteria:
  - **Pass:** At least one email is configured for each necessary category or for the ALL category.
  - **Fail:** Missing emails for any of the notification categories and none configured for the ALL category.
  
This approach ensures that the organization has designated contacts who will be notified for key events and actions by Google Cloud services, supporting compliance and operational awareness.
